name: Lyon Arboretum
tagline: A 194-acre rainforest research garden in upper Manoa Valley — quiet trails, rare tropical plants, and the feel of deep jungle.
parking: Limited lot; arrive early or carpool
bestTime: Weekday mornings for solitude; expect afternoon showers
talkStory: Founded in 1918 by Harold Lyon, a plant pathologist who wanted to test whether reforesting Hawaii's denuded watersheds could restore rainfall — and it worked. The 194-acre UH research station preserves over 5,000 tropical species, including some of the last wild specimens of critically endangered Hawaiian plants. It sits at the head of one of Hawaii's wettest valleys (150+ inches/year), so bring an umbrella. Reservations required — book online.
